ackerschaft.long_description: We create unique, hands-on experiences around the field with the aim of promoting an appreciation of nature and food among children, young people and adults alike.
ackerschaft.field_of_activity: Education, sustainability, environmental protection, nutrition, health, agriculture, garden, gastronomy

projects.title: GemüseAckerdemie
projects.summary: Curriculum-aligned school and kindergarten gardens across Liechtenstein.
projects.problem: Many children grow up disconnected from how food is grown.
projects.intervention: Six schools and four kindergartens receive setup support, training, and long-term integration of vegetable gardens into everyday school life.
projects.outcome: Children and teachers experience the work and joy behind regional food through hands-on gardening.
highlights_for_matchers: Multi-award-winning German programme adapted for Liechtenstein
Strong education + environment crossover for open mandates

track_record.title: Pilot schools 2022–2024
track_record.result: Gardens integrated into weekly lessons; teacher feedback 4.6/5

projects.title: Off to the post-harvest
projects.summary: Public harvest campaigns with Liechtenstein farmers to save field surplus.
projects.problem: Large quantities of vegetables remain in fields after commercial harvest.
projects.intervention: Coordinated post-harvest days with farmers; volunteers collect and distribute produce.
projects.outcome: Food loss reduced; 600+ households on the vegetable-saver mailing list.
highlights_for_matchers: Visible local impact — food waste & climate

projects.title: AckerKüche
projects.summary: Radically regional catering using post-harvest and local ingredients.
projects.problem: Institutional events rarely showcase Liechtenstein produce.
projects.intervention: Two association chefs cook exclusively with regional and rescued vegetables at events.
projects.outcome: Demonstrates viable local food culture; revenue partially reinvested in programmes.
highlights_for_matchers: Earned-income component lowers net funding need

demenz.long_description: Demenz Liechtenstein raises awareness, supports people living with dementia and their families, and works toward a dementia-friendly society — the only organisation in the Principality with this dedicated portfolio.
demenz.field_of_activity: Dementia awareness, carer support, day programmes, outreach

projects.title: Day centre & structured activities
projects.summary: Weekly programme for people with dementia and respite for family carers.
projects.problem: Family carers in LI lack affordable structured daytime support.
projects.intervention: Run a day centre with therapeutic activities and trained staff.
projects.outcome: Improved wellbeing for participants; carers can maintain employment.
highlights_for_matchers: Only provider with this full portfolio in Liechtenstein

projects.title: Awareness & outreach
projects.summary: Community talks, materials, and dementia-friendly community projects.
projects.intervention: School and employer outreach, information events, volunteer network.
projects.outcome: Earlier referrals and more dementia-friendly institutions.

eltern-kind-forum.long_description: The Eltern Kind Forum strengthens families through early childhood programmes, parenting support, and community spaces — core services are often contract-funded; new offers depend on third-party project grants.
eltern-kind-forum.field_of_activity: Early childhood, parenting education, family counselling

projects.title: New family programme pilot
projects.summary: Innovative group offers for parents with young children (third-party funded).
projects.problem: Core operations are secured; innovation requires external grants.
projects.intervention: Pilot bilingual parent-child courses and outreach to underserved municipalities.
projects.outcome: Expand reach beyond existing centres; evaluate for state co-funding.
highlights_for_matchers: Clear split: fundable project vs. general org
